spec_match.rationale: Page runs script and renders dynamic UI, but the visible API surface (window.) only partially mirrors what the feature summary calls out — the headline behaviour isn't unambiguously demonstrated.

interactive.rationale: Some controls present but the interactive surface is thin (9 buttons / 0 inputs / 0 selects). Hard to call it hands-on.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (appendhtml, innerhtml, methods, sanitized, sethtml).

edge_cases.rationale: Page references 14 edge case / failure / fallback hits and the control surface has room to exercise distinct branches, so the happy path is not the only one shown.

references.rationale: Page does not include the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link — violates the rule #3 self-heal invariant.

design_system.rationale: All colours come via 73 CSS-variable references, no raw hex, no button background override without a colour override, and not a static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Tighten the link between the demo and the spec's headline behaviour
openQuestions.detail: Confirm the on-screen interaction maps to a concrete API call from the feature summary on chromestatus: "Expose multiple HTML setting methods that provide a coherent story for dynamically inserting markup into an existing document.  - Positional methods (before/after/append/prepend/replaceWith) that take". If the call is being shimmed or simulated, surface that fact in the page and add a real invocation behind a feature-detect.

openQuestions.title: Expand the interactive surface beyond one or two buttons
openQuestions.detail: Add controls that vary the parameters the spec exposes — toggles, ranges, selects — so the user can drive the demo through several distinct states instead of a single one-shot trigger.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link
openQuestions.detail: Page is missing the chromestatus.com/feature/5054329641893888 link inside a `references` section. CLAUDE.md rule #3 requires this on every concept so the slug-recovery script can re-derive the canonical name.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("Expose multiple HTML setting methods that provide a coherent story for dynamically inserting markup into an existing document.  - Positional methods (before/after/append/prepend/replaceWith) that take HTML as argument, effectively replacing") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://github.com/WICG/declarative-partial-updates/blob/main/dynamic-markup-revamped-explainer.md#resulting-api to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~9351 chars of script across 9 button(s) and 0 other control(s). hits 3 pass / 2 partial / 1 fail across the six rubric criteria.
